en.bio:
I'm a Software Engineer currently at Helsing.

I hold a Master's Degree in Information Systems from the Technical University of Munich and have been writing software since the late 90s. I tend to work on systems with hard operational constraints - domains where the underlying physics shape the design as much as the software does.

Before Helsing, I spent six years at AWS, working on ground segment automation and scheduling for AWS Ground Station, alongside broader work on distributed, data-intensive cloud architectures.

Rust is my primary language these days, but I also work in Python and TypeScript. Across all areas, I contribute to open-source, and occasionally speak at tech conferences.
